section.title: AC/DC Switch
section.paragraph: User can use AC/DC switch to simulate a battery plug in. It is useful when users only plug in AC but want to validate some battery related feature.

list_item.paragraph: X86 - Main processors executing the x86 Instruction Set Architecture
SBIOS - System Basic Input Output System
ACPI - Advanced Configuration and Power Management Interface

section.title: Feature Execution Flow
section.paragraph: BIOS can send the AC/DC switch config to EC through ECRAM, and EC will report fake AC/DC status to BIOS.
User can enable the feature in Device Manager -> AMD PBS -> EC/PD Configurations

section.title: Feature Implementation Details
section.paragraph: If AC DC Switch is enabled by CMOS, then report battery always exist to OS.

Both AC supply and battery are plugged in. AC time and DC time are not zero. Battery capacity > 10%. The switch will be between AC power and actual Battery. If battery capacity < 10%, EC will stop the switch, stay in AC mode, wait battery capacity over 20%, and start the switch again.
Only AC power supply exists. AC time and DC time are not zero. The switch will be between AC power and faked battery, and all use AC power.

section.title: Firmware Interface
section.paragraph: EC to SBIOS interface for AC/DC Switch.

section.title: Dependencies
section.paragraph: Need to plug in AC adapter. It’s better not to plug in real battery when use AC/DC switch because it’s mainly designed for internal user who don’t own one real battery.
